algorithms.title: Adult 1-Rescuer BLS (CPR) Algorithm
algorithms.description: The single-rescuer sequence for an adult in cardiac arrest, following the C-A-B approach.
steps: Verify scene safety and confirm cardiac arrest (unresponsive, no normal breathing, no pulse in ≤10 s).
Activate the emergency response system and get an AED (use a phone on speaker if alone).
Place the patient supine on a firm, flat surface and expose the chest.
Begin compressions: heel of one hand on the lower half of the sternum, other hand on top, rate 100–120/min, depth 2–2.4 inches, full recoil after each.
After 30 compressions, if trained and willing, open the airway (head-tilt/chin-lift) and give 2 breaths, each over ~1 second with visible chest rise.
If untrained or unwilling to ventilate, perform continuous compression-only CPR instead.
Continue cycles of 30:2, minimizing interruptions to less than 10 seconds.
Apply and use the AED as soon as it arrives; resume compressions immediately after any shock.
Switch compressors about every 2 minutes if another rescuer is available.
